canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, Fidelity is predominantly defined in the context of generative models, where it refers to the quality of generated outputs. This common usage characterizes Fidelity as the degree to which outputs are "realistic, high-quality, and free from artifacts," with one paper specifying this as "Image fidelity" to describe the absence of "unintended distortions." In this context, it is discussed as a desirable property of generation systems, with one study noting its method "generates images with greater fidelity." A distinct and less frequent usage appears in the context of model interpretability, where Fidelity is framed differently. In this alternative view, from "Sparse Autoencoders for Hypothesis Generation," the construct is defined as "the accuracy with which a natural language concept captures the activation pattern of a neuron." This paper provides a specific operationalization, measuring the "fidelity of the interpretation" using a formula based on precision and recall.
papers: Position: We Need An Algorithmic Understanding of Generative AI
Robust Multi-bit Text Watermark with LLM-based Paraphrasers
The Emperor’s New Clothes in Benchmarking? A Rigorous Examination of Mitigation Strategies for LLM Benchmark Data Contamination
Localize, Understand, Collaborate: Semantic-Aware Dragging via Intention Reasoner
Multimodal Large Language Models Make Text-to-Image Generative Models Align Better
Neural Residual Diffusion Models for Deep Scalable Vision Generation

definitions.text: The degree to which generated outputs are realistic, high-quality, and free from artifacts.
definitions.paper: Neural Residual Diffusion Models for Deep Scalable Vision Generation

definitions.text: The degree to which a generated image is realistic, high-quality, and free from artifacts or unintended distortions.
definitions.paper: Localize, Understand, Collaborate: Semantic-Aware Dragging via Intention Reasoner

relationships.paper: The Emperor’s New Clothes in Benchmarking? A Rigorous Examination of Mitigation Strategies for LLM Benchmark Data Contamination
